action_items.issue: Dead feature ratio of 89-99% invalidates trained-SAE results
action_items.action: Investigate and fix SAE training configuration. Retrain TopK SAEs with: (a) 10M+ tokens instead of 1M, (b) lower learning rate (try 1e-4), (c) monitor dead feature ratio during training. Target <50% dead features. If dead features cannot be reduced, exclude trained-SAE experiments from the paper.

action_items.issue: E1 architecture comparison confounds architecture with training data/width
action_items.action: Retrain JumpReLU from scratch on OpenWebText with d_SAE=16,384 to match TopK conditions. Alternatively, reframe the paper to focus on the sparsity sweep (E2/E3) and demote the architecture comparison to an observation.
Fix Abstract: 'improves reconstruction MSE by 11.1%' -> 'improves per-pair residual MSE by 11.1% with 388 total parameters.' Ensure this matches the paper text.

action_items.issue: H2-H4 null results may reflect underpowered design rather than true null effects
action_items.action: Add confidence intervals for all correlation coefficients. Report minimum detectable effect size. Replace post-hoc power analysis with equivalence testing or Bayesian analysis. Soften claims from 'collision rate is a poor proxy' to 'collision rate did not correlate with first-letter probing accuracy in our underpowered study.'

action_items.issue: Single seed with no replication; pilot-full discrepancy unexplained
action_items.action: Run E1 and E3 with at least 3 seeds (42, 123, 456) and report standard errors. Explain the 8x discrepancy between pilot (30.8%) and full (3.8%) TopK collision rates.

action_items.issue: Collision rate is not validated as a proxy for true absorption
action_items.action: Implement Chanin et al. absorption detection on first-letter hierarchies (vowel -> {a,e,i,o,u}) for at least one SAE. Report correlation between collision rate and true absorption rate. If correlation is weak, rename CAAB to 'Cross-Architecture Collision Benchmark.'

action_items.issue: Gemma-2-2B fallback explanation is buried in Section 3.3
action_items.action: Add explicit statement in Introduction: 'All experiments use GPT-2 Small (124M parameters); planned Gemma-2-2B experiments were blocked by SAELens API compatibility issues.' Remove Gemma-2-2B from Abstract.

action_items.issue: UAD validated on only 25 first-letter collisions
action_items.action: Validate UAD on at least one additional concept domain (e.g., numbers 0-9). Report precision-recall curves across threshold values. If additional validation is infeasible, demote UAD from a contribution to 'exploratory direction.'

action_items.issue: DFDA evaluated on only 4 pairs sharing one feature
action_items.action: Test DFDA on at least 10 distinct shared features. Report generalization to unseen pairs. If infeasible, demote DFDA from a contribution to 'exploratory direction.'

action_items.issue: Specificity scores in Table 1 are numerical artifacts
action_items.action: Remove specificity scores from Table 1 or replace with a bounded metric (clipped at 100). The current values (6.56e8) are driven by division-by-near-zero and are not interpretable.

action_items.issue: Post-hoc power analysis is methodologically questionable
action_items.action: Replace Section 3.6 post-hoc power statement with confidence intervals for correlation coefficients. Report minimum detectable effect size for 80% power given n=5-6.

action_items.issue: Conclusion overgeneralizes from first-letter probing to 'absorption harm'
action_items.action: Soften Conclusion claim: 'Collision rate is a poor proxy for absorption harm' -> 'Collision rate did not correlate with first-letter sparse probing accuracy on GPT-2 Small, suggesting limited predictive value for this specific downstream task.'
Replace '[anonymous repository]' placeholder with actual repository URL or remove the placeholder entirely.
Update glossary: line 78 'training-free' -> 'SAE-retraining-free'; line 136 'pre-trained' -> 'pretrained' to match actual paper usage.
